task.title: Build the HTML5 output with sidebar TOC
task.shortdesc: Run a local HTML5 build and verify chunking, navigation, filtering (DITAVAL variants), and customization hooks.

prereq.p: You need DITA-OT 4.x installed and accessible as dita.

context.p: This task exercises common task structures and shows how steps can be anchored for deep-linking and interactive UI.
Admin note: use a properties file checked into source control, and build in strict mode in CI.
Novice note: start with the default build, then try one variant at a time so you can verify filtering.

step.cmd: Install the plug-in into your DITA-OT instance.

info.p: Copy or symlink plugins/com.acme.html5.sidebar into your DITA-OT plugins/ directory, then run:
Web hint: confirm the TOC is embedded in each page and the current page is highlighted in the sidebar.

step.cmd: Build one or more filtered variants using DITAVAL.

info.p: This repo includes example DITAVAL files for platform and audience. Each variant is a separate properties file.

step.cmd: Verify the output structure and navigation.

substep.cmd: Open out/html5/index.html and confirm the TOC is present.
Open a topic page and confirm a sidebar TOC is embedded and sticky.
Confirm the current page is highlighted in the TOC.
Compare WEB vs PRINT outputs to confirm filtering is working.

result.p: You have a chunked HTML5 output with an embedded sidebar TOC, filtering variants, and starter customization hooks.
